---
title: 'Isotope Codes: Carbonates (Puerto Rico)'
author: "Bryan Rodriguez"
date: "2025-03-23"
output: html_document
---



```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
rm(list = ls())
setwd("~/OneDrive - University of Kansas/Bryan-MS_PhD/PHD-PROJECT/PuertoRico/CHAPTER3/Isotopes/R_plotting")

```



```{r packages, include = F, echo = FALSE}
library(ggplot2)
library(ggsci)
library(RColorBrewer)
library(plotly)
library(dplyr)
library(tibble)
library(purrr)
library(ggpubr)
library(lubridate)
library(patchwork)
library(readxl)
library(viridis)
library(RColorBrewer)
library(openintro)
library(ggdist)
library(htmlwidgets)
library(sf)
library(ggspatial)
library(tidypaleo)
library(colorspace)
```


#Load Data

```{r Load data}

#Carbonate Data
Guanica_CarbData <- read.csv("InputData/Isotope_Data_Guanica.csv")

#Fix interger chr in depth column
IntergerColumns <- c("DepthCore_m")
Guanica_CarbData <- Guanica_CarbData %>% mutate_if(names(.) %in% IntergerColumns, as.numeric)


```

#Plotting

```{r Defined variables for plotting}

# Generate distinct colors for C2 (blue shades) and C3 (orange shades)
set.seed(42)
c2_colors <- sample(brewer.pal(8, "Blues")[4:8], size = 5)
c3_colors <- sample(brewer.pal(8, "Oranges")[4:8], size = 4)

# Assign colors and shapes
zone_colors <- c(
  # C2 zones (shades of blue)
  "ZC2E" = c2_colors[1], "ZC2D" = "black", "ZC2C" = c2_colors[3], 
  "ZC2B" = "#4DAAE9", "ZC2A" = c2_colors[5],

  # C3 zones (shades of orange)
  "ZC3D" = "#8F3361", "ZC3C" = c3_colors[3], "ZC3B" = c3_colors[2], "ZC3A" = "#C65639",
  
  # Other groups (fixed colors)
  "PLDolMixed" = "purple", "PLCalMixed" = "red", "PLDolTreated" = "orchid",
  "SandMixed" = "grey28",
  "PS_Top" = "green", "PS_BlackL" = "darkgreen"
)

zone_shapes <- c(
  # C2 zones (unique shapes, new shape for ZC2A: diamond)
  "ZC2E" = 18, "ZC2D" = 20, "ZC2C" = 2, "ZC2B" = 1, "ZC2A" = 15,  # Diamond shape for ZC2A
  
  # C3 zones (unique shapes, keeping ZC3B the same)
  "ZC3D" = 8, "ZC3C" = 7, "ZC3B" = 6, "ZC3A" = 5,
  
  # Other groups (consistent shapes)
  "PLDolMixed" = 19, "PLCalMixed" = 19, "PLDolTreated" = 19, # Circles for PL
  "SandMixed" = 12,
  "PS_Top" = 17, "PS_BlackL" = 17 # Filled triangles for microbialites
)


#If plotting with respect to minerals
min_colors <- c("carbonate" = "blue", "gypsum" = "orange", "dolomite" = "red", "calcite" = "gray", "aragonite" = "purple", "mg-calcite" = "brown")
min_shapes <- c("carbonate" = 15, "gypsum" = 17, "dolomite" = 20, "calcite" = 11, "aragonite" = 18, "mg-calcite" = 19)

```


```{r Oficial plot by Zones for paper}


# Plot
Sed_Carbs_Guanica <- ggplot(Guanica_CarbData, aes(x = d18O, y = d13C, color = Identifier_3, shape = Identifier_3)) +
  geom_point(size = 3.5, stroke = 1, na.rm = TRUE) + # Black stroke for clarity
  scale_color_manual(values = zone_colors) +
  scale_shape_manual(values = zone_shapes) +
  xlab(expression(paste(delta^{18}, O[phantom(0)][carb], " ", "(‰, VPDB)"))) +  # X-axis label
  ylab(expression(paste(delta^{13}, C[phantom(0)][carb], " ", "(‰, VPDB)"))) +  # Y-axis label
  ggtitle(expression(paste(delta^{13}, C[phantom(0)][carb], " ", "and", " ", delta^{18}, O[phantom(0)][carb], " ", "Values of different samples in Laguna Providencia and Montalva, Guánica, PR" ))) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    legend.position = "right", 
    legend.key.size = unit(0.5, "cm"), 
    legend.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 12),  
    legend.text = element_text(hjust = 0.5), 
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.15, size = 18, face = "bold"), 
    axis.title.x = element_text(size = 14),
    axis.title.y = element_text(size = 14),
    plot.background = element_rect(fill = "white"),
    panel.grid = element_blank(),         # Remove all grid lines
    panel.border = element_rect(          # Add a square border
      colour = "black", fill = NA, linewidth = 0.5
    )
  ) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "gray40") + # Solid axis lines
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "gray40") +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(-6, 3, by = 1)) + 
  scale_y_continuous(breaks = seq(-12, 5, by = 1)) +
  coord_cartesian(xlim = c(-6, 3), ylim = c(-12, 5)) + # Adjust plot limits
  labs(color = "Legend") +
  labs(shape = "Legend") +
  labs(fill = "Legend")





# Display the plot
Sed_Carbs_Guanica

print("Plot ready to be modified on Illustrator")




```


```{r Make interactive plot}

Interactive_Sed_Carbs_Guanica <- ggplot(Guanica_CarbData, aes(x = d18O, y = d13C, color = Identifier_3, shape = Identifier_3, text =Identifier_2, label = Mineralogy)) +
  geom_point(size = 2.5, na.rm = T) +
  scale_color_manual(values = zone_colors) +
  scale_shape_manual(values = zone_shapes) +
  xlab("d18O carb") +  # Set x-axis label
  ylab("d13C carb") +  # Set y-axis label
    ggtitle("Carbonate Isotope values across Laguna Providencia, Guánica, PR" ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(legend.position = "right", legend.key.size = unit(0.5, "cm"), legend.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 12),  legend.text = element_text(hjust = 0.5), plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.15, size = 18), axis.title.x = element_text(size = 14), axis.title.y = element_text(size = 14), plot.background =   element_rect(fill = "white"), panel.border = element_rect(colour = "black", fill = NA, linewidth = 0.5)) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "solid", color = "gray40") + # Solid axis lines
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "solid", color = "gray40") + # Solid axis lines
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(-6, 3, by = 1)) + # Labels at every 1 interval
  scale_y_continuous(breaks = seq(-12, 5, by = 1)) + # Labels at every 1 interval
  coord_cartesian(xlim = c(-6, 3), ylim = c(-12, 5)) +
  labs(color = "Legend") +
  labs(shape = "Legend") +
  labs(fill = "Legend") +
  guides(fill=guide_legend(title="Legend"))

#Make ggplotly
Interactive_Sed_Carbs_Guanica <- ggplotly(Interactive_Sed_Carbs_Guanica, tooltip = c("x", "y", "color", "text", "label"))

Interactive_Sed_Carbs_Guanica

```



```{r Saving Plots}


# Save as an interactive HTML file
saveWidget(Interactive_Sed_Carbs_Guanica, "plots/Interactive_Sed_Carbs_Guanica.html", selfcontained = T, libdir = NULL)
print("Interactive HTML file Interactive_Sed_Carbs_Guanica saved")


#Save as SVG (for Illustrator editing)
ggsave("plots/Sed_Carbs_Guanica.svg", Sed_Carbs_Guanica, width = 6, height = 6, units = "in", device = "svg")
print("SVG file saved as Sed_Carbs_Guanica.svg")


# Save as TIFF
ggsave(filename = "plots/Sed_Carbs_Guanica.tiff", plot = Sed_Carbs_Guanica, device = "tiff", width = 13, height = 13, dpi = 600, compression = "lzw")
print("TIFF file saved as Sed_Carbs_Guanica.tiff")

```
